findings.description: Repeated footer content inserted between content sections disrupts reading order. The page footer ('State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Energy, Mineral and Land Resources / 512 N. Salisbury Street…') and a decorative colored bar appear inline between the end of the memo cover content (outline I–IV) and the start of Section I (History/Background).
findings.evidence: PDF: Memo cover page ends with the outline (I–IV), and Section I begins on a new page with no inline footer. HTML: After the outline items, the footer text and a colored bar are rendered inline before the 'I. History/Background' heading, creating extra non-content material in the reading flow.

findings.description: Second image uses generic placeholder alt text 'Document background graphic', which provides no meaningful information about the image content.
findings.evidence: HTML: — 'graphic' is explicitly listed as a generic placeholder term that fails accessibility intent.
